summary_one_line: A gAMA chunk whose declared length field is not exactly 4 takes the invalid-length error branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The 4-byte big-endian length field preceding a gAMA chunk (input offsets ~0x21-0x24, immediately before the 'gAMA' fourcc at 0x25-0x28) must hold a value other than 4, so png_handle_gAMA receives length != 4 and takes the true (invalid) branch at pngrutil.c:1136.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: cmplog seeds reach the true branch (resolved=10/10); the per-offset byte diff shows winner inputs carry non-4 length bytes immediately ahead of the gAMA tag (e.g. offset 0x23 holds 0x80/0x01 in 5 winner seeds where the loser is uniformly 0x00), forcing length != 4. I2SRandReplace substitutes the logged comparand for the gAMA length CMP into the matching input offset.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive never resolves (blocked=10/10); its blocking seeds keep the canonical length value at the gAMA length offsets (offset 0x23 is 0x00 across all 10 loser samples and offset 0x24 is 0x04), so length stays at the valid value 4 and the false branch is taken. Without I2S, havoc rarely hits the exact length offset with a non-4 value that still parses to the gAMA chunk.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver entry for the length-vs-4 comparison and splices a differing operand into the input bytes at the offset that fed the comparison, deterministically producing length != 4; naive has only havoc/token mutators and no CMP introspection.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same program-side condition: a gAMA chunk reached with length != 4. The feature axis distinguishing the winner here is not byte-solving but the call-context under which the gAMA handler edge is recorded as new coverage.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves 10/10 with avg blocked duration only 0.90h. Its CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so reaching png_handle_gAMA via a distinct call path registers as a new (call-context, edge) bucket, retaining intermediate corpus entries that bring the fuzzer to the gAMA length comparison and let havoc flip the length field off 4.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks 10/10 for the full 24.00h. With bare per-edge buckets it does not retain the context-distinct intermediates that lead to the gAMA length check, so it lacks the corpus scaffolding to mutate the length field to a non-4 value and stays on the false branch.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's context-sensitive coverage (CtxHook installs a running call-context hash folded into the edge-map index) makes (call-context, edge) pairs count as new coverage, growing the corpus along the path to the gAMA length check; naive's plain MaxMapFeedback over bare edges does not.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the gAMA length check whose globally blocked side is the true branch.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
Source line:    if (length != 4)
Globally blocked side: T  (true branch)

evidence_trail.claim: The gAMA fourcc sits at input offset 0x25-0x28, so the preceding 4 bytes are the chunk length field.
Winner inputs carry non-zero length bytes at offset 0x23 where losers are uniformly zero, forcing length != 4.
cmplog resolves the true branch in every trial while naive blocks in every trial.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
winner: resolved=10/10  blocked=0  unreached=0
  loser:  resolved=0/10  blocked=10  unreached=0

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of naive's mutators, substituting a logged CMP operand into matching input offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S and relies only on havoc plus token mutators.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx resolves the branch in all trials with much lower blocked duration than naive.
naive_ctx folds the call context into the edge-map index so the same edge is recorded at different slots by call path.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx's new-coverage signal is a new (call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
a "new bucket" is a new
(call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 3965 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed <ID>` on three winning cmplog seeds (03ee3f6911e87041, 016607d5919b9103, 10ce63a129aacb82); each ancestor chain contained exactly one I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depths 11, 18 and 10 respectively. The dash signal is I2S-exclusive against naive (the pair-1 loser), confirming an I2SRandReplace contribution in the winner lineage.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic gAMA-length harness in which cmplog fails to beat naive at flipping the 4-byte length field off the value 4 (no cmplog>naive divergence), which would refute the I2S-substitution attribution for pair 1.

weakest_evidence_point: The exact input offset of the length field is inferred from the gAMA fourcc position and the concentrated byte-diff at 0x23; the harness may carry leading offsets that shift the true length-field offset slightly.
